diagrams.title: Half Adder Circuit
diagrams.description: A Half Adder is the fundamental building block for binary addition. It takes two single-bit binary inputs (A and B) and produces a Sum output (S) and a Carry output (C). This diagram shows the gate-level implementation using XOR and AND gates.
elements: XOR gate connecting A and B to produce Sum
AND gate connecting A and B to produce Carry
diagrams.purpose: Explains the basic mechanism of binary addition at the gate level, demonstrating how two 1-bit numbers are added to produce a sum and carry bit.

diagrams.title: Full Adder Circuit
diagrams.description: A Full Adder extends the Half Adder by including a Carry-in input, allowing it to add three 1-bit inputs (A, B, and Carry-in). This enables cascading multiple full adders to create multi-bit adders. The diagram shows both the gate-level implementation and block diagram representation.
elements: Two XOR gates for sum calculation
Two AND gates for carry logic
One OR gate for final carry output
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how addition with carry propagation works, which is essential for building multi-bit adder circuits.

diagrams.title: 4-bit Binary Adder-Subtractor
diagrams.description: This diagram shows a 4-bit Ripple Carry Adder constructed by cascading four full adders. It also demonstrates how the same circuit functions as a subtractor when the Subtract control input is activated, which inverts the B inputs (creating 1's complement) and adds 1 (via Carry-in) to perform 2's complement subtraction.
elements: Four sets of full adder blocks (FA0-FA3)
XOR gates for B input inversion (controlled by M)
Carry-in to LSB (set to M for subtraction)
Carry-out chain from each full adder
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates how multi-bit binary addition and subtraction work using the 2's complement method, showing the complete adder-subtractor circuit used in ALU implementations.
